explanations.2: This loads the bats-support library, which gives you helper functions for testing.
explanations.3: This loads the bats-assert library, which provides assertion commands like assert_failure and assert_success.
explanations.5: This runs the actual shell script we're testing so we can call its functions.
explanations.9: The 'run' keyword executes the function and captures its exit code and output for us to check.
explanations.10: This asserts that the function failed (returned a non-zero exit code), which is what we expect when the path isn't set.
explanations.14: We're setting environment variables that the env::assertFileExists function will use.
explanations.16: The touch command creates an empty file at that path so the test has something to check for.
explanations.17: We run the function again, but this time with the file actually existing.
explanations.18: This asserts that the function succeeded (returned zero exit code) because the file is now there.
